target_population.description: Non-institutionalized adults age 18 or older residing in the United States who can complete an English-language online self-administered survey and are reachable through the contracted online panel frame.
target_population.geography: United States and District of Columbia
inclusion_criteria: Age 18 or older at eligibility screening.
Current residence in the United States or District of Columbia.
Able to complete the ANX-Bench instrument in English without interviewer assistance.
Provides informed consent before exposure to somatic and ambient AI anxiety items.
exclusion_criteria: Under age 18 or unable to verify adult eligibility.
Residence outside the United States or District of Columbia.

Duplicate panel identifier, confirmed vendor fraud, withdrawal of consent, breakoff before any usable ANX item response, straightlining or speeding exclusion defined in the preregistration, or failed attention check.
coverage_limits: The covered frame excludes adults who cannot access online surveys, cannot complete an English instrument, or are not reachable through the selected professional online panel.
Institutionalized adults, adults with unstable internet access, and adults filtered by panel fraud controls are outside the covered analytic frame.

Population estimates are calibrated descriptive estimates for the covered English-language online adult frame and must not be described as nationally representative probability-sample estimates of all US adults.

sampling_frame.frame_description: A contracted professional online panel with respondent profile data for age, gender, race and ethnicity, education, Census region, employment, occupation, and survey-quality monitoring before ANX-Bench outcome inspection.
known_undercoverage: Adults not enrolled in or reachable by online survey panels.
Adults with limited internet access or low online-survey participation propensity.
Adults excluded by panel identity, duplicate, or fraud-detection systems.
sampling_frame.deduplication_rule: Before final weights are estimated, remove repeated panel identifiers, confirmed duplicate device or fingerprint records when available, vendor-confirmed fraudulent completes, and any respondent linked to a prior usable Wave 2 complete.

recruitment_source.vendor_or_panel: Professional online panel vendor selected before Wave 2 fielding
recruitment_source.recruitment_mode: Quota-monitored panel invitation to eligible adults, with study-specific screening completed before any somatic and ambient AI anxiety item is administered.
recruitment_source.eligibility_screening: Screening confirms age, US residence, English self-administration ability, informed consent, duplicate status, and preregistered quality-control eligibility.
recruitment_source.nonresponse_tracking: Archive invitation counts, starts, screenouts, quota stops, breakoffs, consent withdrawals, quality exclusions, complete interviews, final eligible analytic cases, and exclusion reasons by quota variable where vendor reporting permits.

quota_variables.margin_source: Current Population Survey or ACS adult margins fixed before weighting
CPS adult margins for woman and man categories with nonbinary or another identity monitored descriptively
ACS adult race and ethnicity margins fixed before weighting
ACS or CPS adult educational attainment margins fixed before weighting
Census adult regional distribution fixed before weighting
CPS labor-force margins when category mapping is stable; otherwise monitored only
No authoritative external population margin; monitored for sample composition and sensitivity analyses only
No authoritative external population margin; monitored for sample composition and sensitivity analyses only

planned_n_by_split.role: Primary Wave 2 somatic and ambient calibration sample after preregistered exclusions.

construction_steps: Begin with one base weight for each eligible respondent because the nonprobability panel frame does not provide known unequal selection probabilities.
Apply all preregistered eligibility and quality exclusions before estimating final analytic weights.
Rake eligible respondents to external adult margins for age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, and Census region.
Evaluate employment status as an optional calibration variable only when the frozen category mapping aligns with external CPS margins before outcome inspection.
Keep prior AI exposure and AI-news exposure as monitoring and sensitivity variables, not raking variables, unless an external benchmark is frozen before outcome inspection.
Trim weights to the frozen bounds, rescale to mean one, map final_weight to survey_weight, and archive weighted and unweighted diagnostics before psychometric analysis.

survey_weight_mapping.mapping_rule: Copy respondent-level final_weight to survey_weight on every respondent-item row after exclusions, trimming, and mean-one rescaling.

diagnostics: Weighted and unweighted distributions for every quota and monitoring variable.
Weight minimum, maximum, mean, median, coefficient of variation, effective sample size, design effect, and percent trimmed.
Maximum absolute marginal deviation from each external raking target.
Sensitivity table comparing primary weighted estimates with unweighted estimates and with demographic-only weights excluding employment status.

trim_bounds.exception_rule: Weights outside 0.30 to 3.00 are trimmed before rescaling; wider bounds require a frozen addendum before outcome inspection and cannot be selected based on ANX outcome distributions.

variance_design.primary_standard_error: Taylor linearization using final survey_weight for calibrated descriptive estimates; psychometric reliability and factor-model uncertainty are governed by the Wave 2 analysis plan.
variance_design.software: R survey package, version locked in the Wave 2 analysis plan

subgroup_cells.suppression_rule: Suppress or collapse age-group estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or effective sample size below 75.
Suppress sparse gender categories with fewer than 50 eligible respondents and do not use them for confirmatory subgroup difference claims.
Suppress or combine race and ethnicity categories only according to the frozen codebook; report sparse cells as underpowered.
Suppress education subgroup estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or unstable weight diagnostics.
Report AI exposure groups as sample-calibrated descriptive contrasts only because no authoritative population margin is frozen.
Report AI-news exposure groups as descriptive sensitivity strata only, with no population subgroup claim unless external margins are frozen in a later plan.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Weighted and unweighted description of the eligible Wave 2 somatic calibration sample and its covered English-language online adult frame.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Sample description is necessary for interpreting Wave 2 calibration evidence and does not imply probability-sample representativeness.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Calibrated descriptive estimates for covered English-language online US adults, explicitly labeled as nonprobability weighted estimates.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 2 uses a nonprobability online panel, so estimates can support calibrated descriptive claims for the covered online frame but not nationally representative probability-sample claims for all US adults.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Prespecified single-variable subgroup descriptions when minimum cell sizes, suppression rules, and weight diagnostics are satisfied.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The Wave 2 calibration target is construct validation and scored-release calibration, not confirmatory population subgroup hypothesis testing.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No longitudinal trend, change, or repeated-cross-section claim is authorized from Wave 2 alone.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 2 is a calibration wave without a retained-panel follow-up design in this plan.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No event-window, model-release shock, or causal event-study claim is authorized from Wave 2.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The Wave 2 event registry is a no-event calibration registry and contains no exposure-timing design for event-study inference.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No causal effect claim is authorized from Wave 2.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 2 is observational calibration evidence and contains no randomized or quasi-experimental assignment design.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No clinical, diagnostic, screening, treatment, or individual-risk interpretation is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The somatic and ambient ANX-Bench construct is a research measure of AI-related anxiety response, not a validated clinical instrument.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No respondent-level decision, ranking, eligibility, employment, clinical, or policy action may use Wave 2 scores.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: ANX-Bench releases support aggregate research evidence and do not authorize decisions about individual respondents.
